personas.name: Founder-CEO of growth-stage SG SME
personas.icp: Bootstrapped or seed-funded founder-CEO of an SG SME (30-150 headcount, S$5-30M revenue) in tech, professional services, or B2B SaaS who just lost a senior hire to a regional FAANG/Stripe/Anthropic offer and is now under board / co-founder pressure to formalise an ESOP within the quarter. A stranger reading this names: Patsnap (pre-restructure), Ninja Van's vertical spin-outs, GoBear's senior hires, or the typical Antler-Wavemaker-Insignia post-seed SG portfolio company.
pains: Just lost a VP-level hire to 2x cash + RSUs at a regional FAANG/Stripe/Anthropic regional office; cannot match cash, needs equity to compete
Has a phantom-equity Excel sheet the founder maintains alone — co-founder and CFO can't read it without a 30-min walkthrough
Knows ESOP works in theory; doesn't understand IRAS QEEBR mechanics, Section 14CA tax deduction, or CPF treatment of option exercises
Lawyer charges S$350-1,500/hr per grant letter and exercise notice; current ESOP-related legal spend is S$8-15k/yr and rising linearly with headcount
Cap table is cleaner than Carta marketing makes it look but messier than any board would accept — vesting clocks, cliffs, and exercise records are scattered across email and Google Drive
personas.current_workaround: Founder-maintained Excel cap table (one master sheet on the founder's laptop), grant letters drafted ad-hoc by the corporate lawyer at S$350-1,500/hr, exercise notices via email, vesting tracked manually with calendar reminders, and a phantom-equity layer running in parallel for non-tax-resident contractors.

nba.summary: DIY Excel + ad-hoc lawyer billing S$8-15k/yr + 8 hr/mo of founder time on grant admin, vesting reconciliation, and answering 'where do I stand?' questions from key staff.

tooling_lines.tool: Lawyer hourly billing for grant letters / exercise notices (midpoint S$11,500/yr)
Founder time on ESOP admin (8 hr/mo @ S$80/hr loaded)
Excel + Google Drive + DocuSign baseline tooling

personas.name: PE-backed Group HR / Group Finance lead
personas.icp: Group HR Director or Group Finance lead at a Wavemaker / Vertex / Jungle / East Ventures / Insignia / Openspace / Granite Asia portfolio company (50-200 headcount, post-Series-A, S$10-50M revenue) with a board mandate to deliver a defensible cap table for Series B due-diligence within 6-12 months. A stranger names: Glints, Carousell's spin-outs, Carro, Endowus, Aspire, Volopay, Sleek's growth cohort, or any DealStreetAsia 'Series B coming soon' SG headline.
pains: Board has set a fixed Series B due-diligence deadline; cap table cleanup is the gating item the GP keeps flagging in monthly reporting
Phantom-equity grants from the seed era now require retroactive QEEBR registration and Section 14CA treatment; the auditor will not sign without reconstruction

Existing corp-sec firm (BoardRoom, Tricor, Mazars) bills hourly for cap-table reconstruction at S$80-150k for a pre-Series-B cleanup engagement; that bill lands in the deal-cost line and the GP notices
Need scenario-modelling that the Investment Committee can read in a pre-read pack — waterfall, dilution, ratchet outcomes — currently rebuilt in Excel each board cycle
Cross-border employees (SG + ID + VN remote) trigger multi-jurisdiction tax exposure that the in-house team cannot model alone
personas.current_workaround: BoardRoom / Tricor corp-sec retainer with ESOP admin as an hourly add-on (S$6-25k/yr observed range), Excel waterfall model rebuilt by the FP&A analyst before each board pack, KPMG or PwC engaged ad-hoc for the formal annual valuation (S$15-25k/yr), and a separate law-firm retainer for grant agreements.

nba.summary: Steady-state stack of corp-sec ESOP admin + KPMG annual valuation + law-firm grant work + Group HR/Finance lead time on cap-table prep. Pre-Series-B cleanup is a one-off looming cost (S$80-150k) that this stack does not yet pay for but the GP will demand at deal time.

tooling_lines.tool: Corp-sec ESOP admin add-on (BoardRoom/Tricor, S$15k/yr midpoint)
Law-firm grant letter / exercise template work (S$10k/yr midpoint)
Group HR/Finance lead time on cap-table prep (12 hr/mo @ S$120/hr loaded)

personas.name: Family-business succession CFO
personas.icp: CFO or Group Finance Director of a SG family-owned SME (80-200 headcount, S$20-100M revenue, second-generation now active in the business) introducing ESOP to retain 5+ year non-family senior staff during a generational handover. A stranger names: a typical SBF/SCCCI member firm in F&B groups, logistics, manufacturing, or healthcare services — the kind of Pte Ltd that has been in the same family for 30+ years and is now staring down an SGX Catalist listing or a partial PE liquidity event.
pains: Patriarch only just agreed to dilute (often at a family Christmas / CNY dinner); window to formalise is short before he changes his mind
Need a Big-4 / KPMG-grade annual valuation as part of the bundle — a SaaS-only valuation does not survive the family lawyer's review
Family-trust governance interacts with the ESOP pool: ratchet mechanisms, redemption rights, and family-share class protections must be modelled before the patriarch signs
Second-generation successor reads ESOP dilution as 'less for the family' — co-buyer needs a comparison view that PROTECTS family economics, not just optimises for talent retention
Implementation cannot be founder-self-serve; expects a named relationship manager who has done this before with at least one family business of similar profile
personas.current_workaround: Annual KPMG / Mazars / Crowe valuation engagement (S$15-25k/yr), family-office lawyer for trust + share-class structuring (S$8-20k one-off + S$5k/yr maintenance), corp-sec for ACRA filings, and a fragmented set of Excel models the CFO and the successor maintain separately to compare 'with ESOP' vs 'without ESOP' family economics.

nba.summary: Big-4 annual valuation + family-trust legal review + corp-sec premium + CFO time on succession-aware modelling — typical bundled spend for a SG family SME running ESOP discussions.

tooling_lines.tool: Big-4 / KPMG / Mazars annual valuation (S$25k/yr midpoint for S$20-100M revenue band)
Family-office lawyer for trust + share-class + ratchet structuring (S$15k/yr blended one-off + maintenance)
Corp-sec premium tier for family-business statutory complexity (S$8k/yr)
CFO + successor time on parallel 'with vs without ESOP' modelling (40 hr/yr @ S$150/hr loaded)

pricing_models.name: Per-firm flat bundle (annual)
pricing_models.rationale: Aligns with Agent 2's 'sell the bundle, not the seat' implication — at a SAM of S$38M / 3,200 firms, per-seat pricing compresses ARPU below S$3k and shrinks SOM materially. SG SME procurement preference is for single-fee turnkey engagements (BoardRoom Pulse 2024: 78% of SG SMEs that adopted ESOP 2022-24 used single-vendor bundled engagements). This model maps directly to PSG (per-firm cap S$30k) and EDG (per-project) grant arithmetic, which is the structural pricing wedge.

pricing_models.name: Per-employee (per-stakeholder) seat-based
pricing_models.rationale: Standard Carta / Cake / Qapita pattern. Penalises larger headcount which is exactly the wrong incentive for the retention-focused SG ESOP buyer; also doesn't map to PSG's per-firm cap. Competitor research shows public SG dissatisfaction with USD-priced per-seat plans (Indie Hackers SG 2025-11).

pricing_models.name: Hybrid: base platform fee + per-employee overage
pricing_models.rationale: Honest middle when a flat bundle under-captures at the 150-200 headcount end. Adds explanatory burden; the pricing page needs a calculator. Workable for the PE-backed segment where headcount growth is rapid and predictable.

pricing_models.name: One-time setup fee + annual maintenance
pricing_models.rationale: Mirrors the corp-sec / law-firm model SG SMEs already understand. Sets up a clean grant-stacking story (setup fee on EDG / WDG, maintenance on PSG). But buyer fatigue is high once they realise the recurring fee continues; better as a tier variant than a default.

pricing_models.name: Outcome-based (success fee at exit / liquidity event)
pricing_models.rationale: Maximum alignment, minimum adoption — SG SME founders are deeply averse to 'we get paid more if you exit' language because it implies vendor influence on transaction shape. Useful as a 0.25-0.5% transaction-support upsell at deal close, never as the default model.

elasticity_heuristics.evidence: PSG-anchored: SG SME budget threshold for unfamiliar SaaS hardens at the S$30k PSG cap (effective S$15k after grant); above S$15k effective the buyer defers to next quarter or DIYs. Indie Hackers SG 2025-11 thread (8 distinct SG founder commenters) explicitly debated S$200-300/mo as the upper edge of 'casual sign-up' for cap-table tooling; Cake Equity's USD 79-249/mo pricing page is the reference point this persona compares against. Three SG founder interviews (Q1 2026, Elitez sales motion logs) all named 'PSG eligibility' as a hard procurement requirement, not a nice-to-have. Walks at S$15k+ effective. Founder-CEOs in this band have approval authority for unbudgeted spend only up to ~S$10-15k/yr without board/co-founder sign-off.

elasticity_heuristics.segment: PE-backed Group HR / Group Finance lead
elasticity_heuristics.evidence: Deal-blocking risk dominates price sensitivity. Bain SEA PE Report 2025 documents US$32B undeployed PE dry powder targeting SEA with 25-30% landing in SG; for any Series-B-bound portfolio company, the cost of a delayed close (typical 3-6 month delay = S$300k-1M of bridge financing) dwarfs any platform fee under S$60k. Pre-Series-B cleanup engagements observed at S$80-150k are accepted as deal-cost-of-doing-business in 4 of 5 GP-board reporting cycles reviewed in DealStreetAsia coverage 2024-25. The buyer's frame is 'what does the GP think is defensible', not 'is this cheap'. Carta and Qapita both successfully sell into this segment at USD 6-15k/yr without elasticity friction.

Anchors to Big-4 audit + valuation fees (S$15-25k for the annual valuation, S$30-50k for the full advisory engagement) as the reference for what professional services should cost. KPMG SG Private Company Tax Briefing 2024 notes 'family-business CFOs benchmark new vendor costs against existing audit / advisory line items'. Will pay above headline if the bundle includes a Big-4-grade valuation deliverable they can show the patriarch and the family lawyer; will walk if pricing reads 'SaaS-tier-without-the-rigour'. Stephen Riady Centre / NUS Family Firm Survey 2024 (n=210) showed 58% in second-gen-active firms had ESOP discussions stalled — these stalls are recoverable at the right price-and-rigour combination, not at any price.

recommended_tiers.name: Founder Bundle
what_in: Event-sourced cap table SaaS (unlimited stakeholders, SG-resident hosting)
Annual platform-generated valuation refresh (KPMG-comparable methodology, S$5-30M equity range)
Legal templating suite: grant letter, exercise notice, vesting agreement, board resolution, IRAS QEEBR registration pack
IRAS Section 14CA tax-deduction worksheet auto-export (corporate auditor-ready for Form C-S)
Self-serve trading-window clearing engine with PDPA-compliant audit trail
ACRA Annual Return cap-table export (Q1 + Q3 filing season)
PSG-eligible SaaS bundle (S$30k cap, single application via BGP)
what_excluded: Series-B due-diligence pack and IC-grade scenario modelling (upgrade to Series-B Ready)
Bespoke legal review of non-template grant terms (upgrade to Series-B Ready)
Big-4-grade independent valuation deliverable (upgrade to Family Office)
Named relationship manager and family-trust-aware governance (upgrade to Family Office)
recommended_tiers.psychological_anchor: S$12k/yr is the cost of one senior-staff replacement in headhunter fees — and PSG cuts your effective price to S$6k, less than the lawyer bill you're already paying.

recommended_tiers.name: Series-B Ready
what_in: Everything in Founder Bundle, plus:
Series-B due-diligence pack: GP-ready cap-table file, vesting waterfall reconciliation, exercise history audit, multi-jurisdiction tax exposure summary
Board-pack scenario modelling: dilution, ratchet, anti-dilution, preferred-stack waterfall — exportable to IC pre-read PDF
KPMG-equivalent annual independent valuation (S$10-50M equity range, methodology disclosure pack)
Multi-jurisdiction tax templating (SG QEEBR + ID + VN + remote-employee edge cases)
Bespoke legal review of up to 5 non-template grant agreements per year (Elitez Group panel counsel)
Quarterly cap-table-cleanliness review with named CSM
EDG-eligible consulting wrap (Human Capital Development pillar) bundled into engagement scope
what_excluded: Big-4-audited valuation deliverable signed by named partner (upgrade to Family Office)
Family-trust-aware governance templates and ratchet-with-redemption structuring (upgrade to Family Office)
Concierge succession-planning workflows (upgrade to Family Office)
recommended_tiers.psychological_anchor: S$25k/yr is one-fifth of the S$80-150k pre-Series-B cleanup engagement your GP has already priced into deal cost — and PSG + EDG together drop your effective price below S$10k.

recommended_tiers.name: Family Office
what_in: Everything in Series-B Ready, plus:
Big-4-audited annual valuation deliverable (KPMG / Mazars / Crowe partner-signed, family-lawyer-reviewable)
Family-trust-aware governance templates: ratchet mechanisms, redemption rights, family share-class protections, two-tier voting structures
Succession-planning workflow: parallel 'with ESOP' vs 'without ESOP' family-economics modelling for patriarch + successor review
Named relationship manager from Elitez Group (named partner-level, has shipped at least 3 family-business ESOPs)
WSG WDG(JR+) workforce-redesign training wrap for in-house finance team (70% rebate, S$50k cap)
Two-on-two demo flow for founder + designated successor sign-off
Annual family-office governance review with patriarch + successor + family lawyer
what_excluded: M&A transaction-support success fee at deal close (priced separately at deal time, 0.25-0.5%)
recommended_tiers.psychological_anchor: S$60k/yr is half a Big-4 partner's annual fee on this engagement, and EDG + WDG(JR+) drop your effective price under S$20k — less than the family lawyer's bill on a single trust amendment.

grants.name: PSG (Productivity Solutions Grant)
grants.eligibility: SG-registered, ≥30% local shareholding, ≤200 employees AND ≤S$100M annual revenue, purchase for own business use; vendor must be IMDA pre-approved for the relevant solution category. Hard pre-req: Elitez ESOP must apply for PSG pre-approval at IMDA — currently NOT listed (target submission 2026-Q3 to access 2026-Q4 SME budget cycle).

grants.name: EDG (Enterprise Development Grant)
grants.eligibility: SG-registered, ≥30% local shareholding, no headcount cap; project must be submitted via BGP before start under Human Capital Development / Financial Management pillar with measurable outcomes; SMEs in Tier-1 priority sectors get 70% support. Applies to the consulting wrap bundled with the SaaS deliverable, not the subscription alone.

grants.name: WDG(JR+) — Workforce Development Grant Job Redesign Plus
grants.eligibility: SG-registered, ≥30% local shareholding; engagement must be delivered by a WSG pre-approved JR+ consultant (Elitez Group is on the approved list, LOA valid through 17 Mar 2027) and structured as a workforce-redesign project including training of in-house finance/HR team to maintain the platform.

grants.name: SFEC (SkillsFuture Enterprise Credit)
grants.eligibility: SG enterprise with ≥S$750 SDL contributions over qualifying period AND ≥3 SG local employees; stackable on top of PSG/EDG for the training component of an engagement only, not the SaaS subscription itself.
